Alright, you're back. Let's roll forward. Here's the deal. The purpose of this lesson is to begin the process of identifying what your specific limiting beliefs are. That's the first step in cracking them and replacing them with impaling beliefs. So let me just ask you this. Just for the sake of just being general to specific, right? Do you have any limiting beliefs? Yes or no? Answer yes if you have some. Don't be specific. I don't care what they are. Do you have any limiting beliefs that are holding you back or no you don't? Choose one. Bingo. Thank you for being honest. Of course you have limiting beliefs. Some does, my friend. We all have them. Wow. You don't have any limiting beliefs. I have great news for you. You just found your first limiting belief that you don't have any limiting beliefs. My friend, we all have them. I have them. You have them. That's how human beings are built and we get them simply as I said before from moving through life, experiencing things, hearing things, seeing things and the thing is we don't have spyware. We can't block them. They're like viruses that enter our brains from the outside. We have no way of blocking them. We're not built with sort of antivirus software and once they're there, as I said before, they essentially stop you from charging forward when you should cause you to pull back when you shouldn't and I promise you you got them. Even worse, we are all held down by our lowest level limiting beliefs. That's the one that kind of gives us the ceiling of where we can't perform beyond. Perfect metaphor. You could have a beautiful Ferrari, Cherry Red, 12 cylinders, four valves per 550 horsepower, cable going 200 miles an hour, 250 even, right? Beautiful Cherry Red chassis. It's awesome, right? But if you have a governor on the engine stopping the flow of gas, I don't care how fast it could go. The car ain't going over 55. That's how limiting beliefs work. They sit on your life and act as a governor, a ceiling for the things that you want to achieve. So you've got to root them out. There's no other way, bottom line. Now, the first step is to identify specifically what limiting beliefs are holding you back from achieving what you want in terms of success and money and in sales because that's what this training is all about. Just remember that there are beliefs for everything in life, but this is a money-centric, sales-centric training. So what I'm going to do now is put up five limiting beliefs one by one, give you examples of what might be holding you back. Start with number one. If I get rich, I'll become greedy. Now, this is a real doozy. Where would this come from? Well, typically from your parents or someone that you knew when you were very young and perhaps you heard them say it, just picked it up in the background or even maybe you were on the wrong end of this where someone that you loved and cared about neglected you in your own mind at least because they were focused on making money. And the meaning that you took away from that experience was that this person is being greedy and no longer cares about me because they're focused on making money. So if I get rich, I'll become just like that. You get it? Reality? Money is like alcohol. It simply makes you more of what you already are. If someone you know is an a-hole, so to speak, get them drunk, what are they? A giant a-hole. If they're great, awesome, fun-loving people, get them drunk, they are the absolute life of the party. Their awesomeness, their gregariousness can't be contained. They're just amazing, right? Alcohol magnifies who you are and so does money. It doesn't change you. Now let's move on to number two. I am not entrepreneurial. This is a classic one as if somehow you're born lacking a gene for entrepreneurship or just being in business for yourself. I mean, come on. We could almost think away this one because it's so idiotic when we think about it, but unfortunately beliefs don't work that way. You can't just wish them away or think them away. My life, I actually had this for a very short time. Quick story. I want to believe at the point here, but from the time I was a kid, I was basically a born entrepreneur from when I emerged my mother's womb. At the age of eight, I had a paper and I was knocking on doors to expand it. At the age of 10, I was shoveling dryways at the snowstorms in New York. At the age of 12, I was doing kids magic shows. At the age of 16, I hit a big selling ISIS on the beach. I told you that story already, right? All of these things I did, then I go into the meat business at the age of 22, right at 21. And guess what? I go bankrupt. That one bankruptcy inserted the belief in me that maybe I'm not meant to be an entrepreneur. Maybe I'm just a great salesman, so I shouldn't start my own business. And I actually almost didn't start strattin' because of that belief. As I said, people like myself and many people like me, right? We break through those beliefs, but they hold us back for a time. So let me just say that if you think that you're not capable of being an entrepreneur, I promise you that's the one you choose to break. You won't think that when we're done. Okay? Number three, the classic one, selling is evil. Oh boy, okay? I grew up with this one in my household. My parents thought that selling or salespeople was synonymous with these two words, slime and bucket. My parents hated salespeople. Now, just so you know, very often with beliefs, we either will be just like our parents or go the exact opposite way. Well, obviously I went the opposite way. But still, if you have this, just get honest about it. It's a very easy one to crack, but just that's a possibility. Number four, I'll have to sacrifice everything for hard work. Wow. I mean, all I can say is that the hardest working people I know are those that punch a clock for 30 years, every single day into the grind, like a gerbil on one of those spinning wheels. Good. Oh my God. It's like, I mean, I can't believe that people actually think this, but guess what? They do. They do. Very common. And what happens is it's a mistaken notion in the sense that when you first start your own business, you first want to make a lot of money. Yes, there's a time you're going to work really, really extra hard longer hours. But if you know the rules of number two, meaning to be an entrepreneur, and you know how to scale and leverage and delegate, well, then eventually you can pull back and reap the benefits without working all that hard. You get it? But many people have that belief number four, and it's a killer. And number five, the most toxic belief of all, the world happens to me. I am a creature of circumstance, not a creator of circumstance. Three things happen when this is your belief. Number one, you blame, you play the so called blame game. You blame your parents, you blame your teachers, you blame your boss, you blame the government, you blame the economy, you blame everybody, but who? You don't blame yourself. You're exempt, right? Toxic way to live. Number two, you justify, you make excuses. You say, oh, money's really not that important to me. Oh, really? Well, let me just tell you two things. Number one, I want you to try to go to the bank on the first of the month and say to the teller, I'd like to deposit some love to pay the rent. See what she does or he does. They'll take you away in a straight jacket. I mean, honestly, money is the single most important thing in the world for a few things, like number one, food, number two, shelter, number three, medical care. I mean, good Lord. I mean, if you're in a country that give you medical care for three, you're lucky, but you guys still have to pay your rent. You still have to eat. Money's not the be-all end, I'll give you that. Okay? I've been rich and I've been incredibly happy. I've been rich and I've been unhappy. I'll give you that one too. I've been poor and been utterly horribly miserable, but I've never been poor and happy. I can't be and most people can't. When you're poor, it literally complicates everything in your life. Money is the greatest problem solver known to man. When you lack it, I've seen divorces, infighting and marriages, sick children is not be able to get treated. It's just so toxic, so terrible. Here's what you want to do. You want to solve that money problem, solve the money problem and then get on to all the higher great stuff, the contribution, the connection, but you've got to solve the money problem. You get it? And just one more thing, say money is not important to me. Well, you know what? We don't end up possessing things that aren't important to us. I don't have a motorcycle right now. You know why? It's not important to me. You don't like money. You don't want money. It's not important. Guess what? You'll never have any. Universal law, it will avoid you like the plague. So please, if that's your belief, you've got to crack that one. Okay? Number three, the third and hit for it. Oh, the world happens to me. The worst one of all, you complain. You're constantly saying, this is wrong. That's wrong. Oh my God. The most toxic one. Oh, you know why? Because when you complain, what you're doing is you're essentially focusing on everything that's wrong in your life, or the so-called crap of your life, the crap. Well, what you focus on, you attract. So what happens? You become a giant crap magnet. You end up attracting the things that you complain about. I mean, when I hear someone complain, I'm like, get away from you. The crap might hit me on the way to you. So I just, I don't want those people near me. The world happens to me is an awfully toxic belief. You get it? And we're going to have to root it out and replace it with, I make my own way. I am a creator of circuses, Hannibal. I either find a way or I make a way. That's the belief. I have it. I want you to have as well, because that's the beginning of improvement to say, I can make a change. It's not fate. I move for the world that can change my circumstances. I can make the world the way I want it. You create a vision and so forth. And boom, you are set. So those are your five limiting beliefs that are very common. Now, here's what I want you to do. I don't want you to choose your belief yet. I need you to take the next four or five lessons in sequence. And in the beginning of the next lesson, I'm going to have you choose a belief that you want to crack. Here's the deal. If you choose one right now, but don't have time to follow through with the rest of this module, you're doing yourself a huge disservice. I'm going to have all these things fresh in your mind. And then we're going to run a very powerful NLP pad that I learned directly from Dr. Bandler that will allow you to crack this limiting belief with major force and replace it with the exact opposite belief, the powering belief, and your life will soar.
